reasoning: The volume of EU natural gas imports from Russia dropped substantially following the Ukraine conflict. With pipeline deliveries through Ukraine ending in January 2025, Russian gas to the EU arrives primarily via TurkStream (~15 bcm per year) and LNG (~20 bcm per year). Together, this yields ~35 bcm annually, or under 9 bcm per quarter. Reaching 15 bcm in a single quarter of 2025 is exceedingly unlikely given infrastructure constraints, political commitments (REPowerEU), and historical capacity drops. The crowd forecast is likely correctly anchored near 1%.
